question: The planets nearest to Sun are............. (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Mercury and Mars
option_2: Earth and Mercury
option_3: Mercury and Venus
option_4: Mercury and Uranus
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Mercury and Venus are two planets nearest to the Sun. The order of the planets, starting nearest the sun is Mercury, Venus, Earth, Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Uranus, Neptune.

question: You are asked to import ostrich, platypus and koala bear. Which country would you select to go where you get all these three? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Japan
option_2: Australia
option_3: New Zealand
option_4: Canada
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Ostrich, Platypus and Koala bear all three are found in Australia.

question: ‘Golan heights’ belonging to country ‘A’ were captured by a country ‘B’. Which of the following are “A” and “B” respectively? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Syria and Israel
option_2: Israel and Syria
option_3: Syria and Egypt
option_4: Egypt and Israel
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

The Golan Heights belonged to Syria (A) and were captured by Israel (B) during the Six-Day War in 1967. Therefore, the correct option is (a) Syria and Israel. Key details about the conflict: The Golan Heights are a strategically important plateau. Israel captured the territory from Syria during the Six-Day War in June 1967. In 1981, Israel effectively annexed the area, though this action has not been recognized internationally. Syria has continually demanded the return of the territory.

question: Richter scale is used to measure............... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Earthquakes
option_2: Ocean depth
option_3: Intensity of wind
option_4: Temperature of the body
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Richter scale is used to measure earthquakes using Sesimograph.

question: What is a continental shelf? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: It is a part of the ocean which is really an extension of the land mass, but submerged
option_2: It is that part where the ocean commences
option_3: It is a land mass which is surrounded by water on all sides
option_4: It is a part of the continent that is submerged in relatively shallow sea
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Continental Shelf is a part of the continent that is submerged in relatively shallow sea. It is the area of seabed around a large land mass where the sea is  shelf eologically part of the continental crust.

question: In which type of rocks, fossils are more abundantly found? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Igneous
option_2: Metamorphic
option_3: Sedimentary
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • In sedimentary rocks, fossils are found abundantly. These rocks formed by sedimentation such that the remains of past life gets buried in the depositing sediments. Later, as these sediments compress, the remains turn into fossils.

question: International date line............... (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Roughly corresponds to the 180th meridian, the regions which are counted as being one day earlier than regions to the east.
option_2: Roughly corresponds to the 180th meridian, the regions which are counted as being one day earlier than regions to the west.
option_3: Roughly corresponds to 90th meridian which falls side of the Greenwich meridian
option_4: Roughly corresponds to 135th meridian which falls side of the Greenwich meridian
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • The International Date Line (IDL) roughly corresponds to the 180th meridian of longitude. The IDL is an imaginary line that runs from the North Pole to the South Pole, marking the transition from one calendar day to the next.
  • The IDL is the line where the date changes by one day.
  • When crossing the IDL from west to east, the date moves forward by one day.
  • When crossing the IDL from east to west, the date moves backward by one day.
  • The IDL is not a straight line, but follows political boundaries and geographical considerations.
  • The IDL was established in 1884 and runs through the middle of the Pacific Ocean.

question: A narrow strip of land, bordered on both sides by water, connecting to larger bodies of land is called............... (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Dune
option_2: Equinox
option_3: Isthmus
option_4: Strait
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • A narrow piece of land that connects two landmasses is known as an isthmus. It is generally surrounded by water bodies on two of its sides.
  • Best examples : Isthmus of Panama, joining the two Americas and Separating the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. Isthmus of Gibraltar, joining Gibraltar to Spain.

question: Doldrums refer to................ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: A belt of calm and light variable winds near the equator
option_2: A particular area in the centre of the Pacific Ocean
option_3: The region of the upper atmosphere extending upward from the tropopause to about 20 kms above the earth
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Doldrums is a belt of calm and light variable winds near the equator.
  • Doldrums, also called equatorial calms, equatorial regions of light ocean currents and winds within the intertropical convergence zone (ITCZ), a belt of converging winds and rising air encircling Earth near the Equator.

question: Heavy rainfall affects soil by................ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Increasing its acidity
option_2: Increasing its alkalinity
option_3: Reducing its fertility
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Heavy rainfall causes leaching and therefore removes the salts in the soil which balances the soil acidity.
  • Hence, in case of heavy rainfall, the acidity of soil increases and so also its salinity.

question: Heavy rainfall affects soil by................ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Increasing its acidity
option_2: Increasing its alkalinity
option_3: Reducing its fertility
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Heavy rainfall causes leaching and therefore removes the salts in the soil which balances the soil acidity.
  • Hence, in case of heavy rainfall, the acidity of soil increases and so also its salinity.

question: The rising of evening star Venus indicates................. (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: South Pole
option_2: North Pole
option_3: East
option_4: West
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • The Venus rises in the west and sets in the east thus, the rising of evening star would indicate west pole.
  • The orbit of Venus is inside the orbit of Earth. Unlike the outer planets, Venus is always relatively close to the Sun in the sky.

question: Pampas are the vast grassy plains of................ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: South America
option_2: North America
option_3: Africa
option_4: Eurasia
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • The Pampas are fertile South American lowlands.

question: U.S.A. found rings around which planet? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Venus
option_2: Jupiter
option_3: Mars
option_4: Pluto
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • The USA has found rings around the planet of Jupiter. Four of the planets in the Solar System have rings.

They are the four giant gas planets :

1. Jupiter

2. Saturn

3. Uranus

4. Neptune.

question: When a person moves from poles to the equator, the population of plants and animals will............... (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Increase
option_2: Decrease
option_3: Remain unchanged
option_4: Not show any consistent behavior
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Poles have harsh climatic conditions like severe cold, less availability of drinking water, etc. which makes survival tough there.
  • Hence, when a person moves from equator to poles, he finds less biodiversity and reverse is observed if a person moves from poles to equator.

question: Which of the following statements is correct? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Fossil I is heavier than fossil II
option_2: Fossil II is heavier than fossil I
option_3: Fossil I is older than fossil II
option_4: Fossil II is older than fossil I
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Fossil II is older or more primitive than Fossil I.
  • It’s because the Fossil-II in the lower layer has undergone the sedimentation than Fossil-I.

question: Earthquakes are caused due to................ (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Volcanic eruptions
option_2: Landslides
option_3: Cyclones
option_4: Movement of a part of earth’s surface on account of the faulting of rocks
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Earthquakes occur when there is sudden movement of rocks called tectonic plates beneath the surface, which is called “Faulting of rocks”.

question: Why U.S.S.R. has many local time differences? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Because of long east-west stretches
option_2: Because north-south stretch is more
option_3: Because it covers two continents
option_4: Because of large differences in climate
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • The Soviet Union (USSR) had many local time differences because of its long east west stretches.
  • Large countries like the USSR have many time zones because of their vast longitudinal extent.
  • Each time zone covers about 15° of longitude.
  • The USSR had 11 time zones to cover 165° of longitudinal extent.

question: Different seasons are formed because................. (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Sun is moving around the earth
option_2: Of revolution of the earth around the sun on its orbit
option_3: Of rotation of the earth around its axis
option_4: All of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

The first statement is incorrect because the sun does not move around the earth; instead, the earth revolves around the sun. The second statement is correct — the revolution of the earth around the sun, combined with the tilt of the earth’s axis (23½°), is the real reason for the formation of different seasons. As the earth orbits the sun, different parts of the planet receive varying amounts of sunlight during the year, causing summer, winter, spring, and autumn. The third statement is also incorrect because the rotation of the earth around its axis causes day and night, not seasonal changes. Therefore, among the given statements, only the second one is correct, and the correct answer is option (b).

question: Forests help in................. (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Soil erosion
option_2: Soil protection
option_3: Depleting the soil of its moisture
option_4: All of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Forests does not aid in soil erosion but prevent it. Infact they help to protect it to great extent. Forest is often considered as great means to protect soil and conserve it.

question: What are the grass lands of South America called? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Stepes
option_2: Prairies
option_3: Pampas
option_4: Savanna
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The grasslands of South America are called Pampas. The Pampas are fertile grasslands that cover more than 1,200,000 square kilometers. They include the Argentine provinces of Buenos Aires, La Pampa, Santa Fe, Entre Ríos, and Córdoba; all of Uruguay; and Brazil’s southernmost state, Rio Grande do Sul.
  • The Pampas are similar to the North American prairie and are among the chief agricultural areas of South America. In addition to cattle grazing and wheat farming, Argentina also has vineyards in the Pampas.
  • The Pampas are home to many animals, including burrowing owls, pampa deer, gray fox, and guanacos.
  • Steppe grasslands are found in Eastern Europe and Central Asia, and neighboring countries. This region is often referred to as “the Great Steppe”. It stretches from Ukraine in the west through Russia, Kazakhstan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan to the Altai, Koppet Dag, and Tian Shan ranges in China.
  • Prairies are grasslands found in North America, including parts of the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
  • Savanna grasslands are found in Africa, Asia, Europe, Australia, and South America. They are located within 30 degrees of the equator and are often found between tropical rain forests and deserts.
  • Savannas are grasslands with scattered trees. They cover about 20% of the Earth’s land area. Savannas are mostly located in areas with warm to hot climates.

question: There are two places P and Q. The longitudes of P and Q are 45° E and 60°W respectively. What will be the difference in their local times? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: 7 hours
option_2: 6 hours
option_3: 3.5 hours
option_4: Both places will have the same local time
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

The local time of a place changes by 4 minutes for every 1° of longitude. There are two places, P (45° E) and Q (60° W). The total difference in their longitudes is 45° + 60° = 105°. Since each degree of longitude corresponds to 4 minutes of time difference, the total time difference is 105 × 4 = 420 minutes, which equals 7 hours. The place located to the east (P) will have its local time ahead of the place to the west (Q), because the earth rotates from west to east. Hence, the difference in their local times is 7 hours, and place P (45° E) is 7 hours ahead of place Q (60° W).

question: Why is there a severe difference in the climates of Northern and Southern Hemisphere? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Due to rotation of the earth around its axis
option_2: Due to revolution of the earth around the sun of its orbit
option_3: Both of the above
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The Correct answer is both A and B. There are several reasons for the difference in the climates of Northern and southern hemisphere. These are rotation of the earth around its axis and the revolution of the earth around the sun of its orbit.
  • The biggest factor in differences in the climate in the Northern and Southern Hemispheres has to do with the distribution of land vs. ocean. The Northern Hemisphere has much more of Earth’s land masses, while the Southern Hemisphere has a larger fraction of ocean..

question: Humidity of the air............ (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: Increases with the increase in atmospheric temperature
option_2: Decreases with the increase in atmospheric temperature
option_3: is not affected by the change in atmospheric temperature
option_4: Does not show any consistent behaviour with the change in atmospheric temperature
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Temperature is directly proportional to humidity of air. The more temperature of an air is, the more humidity it would have provided that the soil is wet such as in coastal areas. Hence, the correct answer is “A”.

question: Himalayan rivers are perennial because............. (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: They flow from inexhaustible springs in the Himalayas
option_2: They are fed in summer by melting snow of Himalayas
option_3: There is continuous rain throughout the year in the catchment areas
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Himalayan rivers are perennial because they are fed in summer by melting snow of their originating glacier. They pass through the regions which witnesses relatively low rainfall still they are perennial because glaciers continue feeding them throughout the year.

question: “Mulching” the soil is a process whereby................ (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: Big pieces of soil are broken down into smaller pieces
option_2: The field is irrigated at regular intervals
option_3: Transplanting of seedlings take place
option_4: Litters are used to prevent erosion and evaporation
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Mulching is a soil amendment technique that involves covering the topsoil with plant material. The purpose of mulching is to keep the soil covered while farming.

Mulching can:

1. Protect the soil: Mulch protects the soil and returns nutrients to it as it breaks down.

2. Enhance soil organisms: A mulch cover enhances the activity of soil organisms such as earthworms.

3. Prevent erosion: Mulching can prevent erosion and sometimes enrich the soil.

4. Protect roots: Mulching can protect the roots of plants from heat, cold, or drought.

5. Keep fruit clean: Mulching can keep fruit clean.

Organic mulch includes:

  • Compost, Grass clippings, Wood chips, Shredded bark, Straw, Pine needles.
  • Inorganic mulch includes: Black plastic, Geotextiles, Landscape fibers.

question: The tropical deciduous trees shed their leaves in summer................ (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: Leaves get dried up due to the excessive heat
option_2: To prevent excessive loss of water through transpiration
option_3: Because rainfall is inadequate
option_4: Days are longer
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

Tropical deciduous trees shed their leaves in summer as an adaptation to survive the dry and hot conditions typical of that season. During summer, the temperature is high and rainfall is very low, causing a severe shortage of water in the soil. To minimize water loss through transpiration, these trees drop their leaves, which are the main organs of water evaporation. By doing so, they conserve moisture and reduce the demand for water until the rainy season returns. The leaves grow back with the onset of monsoon, when sufficient rainfall replenishes the soil moisture. Thus, shedding leaves in summer helps tropical deciduous trees survive drought-like conditions, making option (b) the correct answer.

question: Flame of the forest is................ (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: A tree blossomed with flowers like flame in leafless season
option_2: Fire developed in the forest due to strong wind
option_3: A lady who worked for the development of the forests
option_4: An insect which glows like a flame in the forest
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Flame tree is a common name used in various parts of the world to refer to several different species of tree which easily catch fire during the course of lightning.

question: The purest form of water is obtained from............. (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: A deep tube well
option_2: A running stream
option_3: Hot water spring
option_4: Heavy rains
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Rain water is considered the purest form of water. Impurities and salts present in water on earth are left behind during vapourisation by the sun.
  • However, the rain water we receive on earth is not necessarily pure, as it initially brings down impurities and particles present in the atmosphere along with it but rain water after prolonged rains is more pure.

question: Contour bunding is used................ (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: To stop the winds in sandy deserts
option_2: To irrigate desert areas
option_3: To prevent erosion in hilly areas
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Contour ploughing or contour farming or Contour bunding (step farming) is the farming practice of ploughing and/ or planting across a slope following its elevation contour lines. It is practiced in hilly areas in order to minimize the soil erosion impacts by downward flowing water.

question: The conclusion that Asia and Africa had a single landmass is provided by............... (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: The deserts in Asia and Africa
option_2: The similarity in climate of certain regions of Asia and Africa
option_3: The finding of same type of fossil in both the regions
option_4: All of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • All of these provide clues about the similarity between two land masses of Africa and Asia.

question: Plankton refers to................. (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: A kind of fish
option_2: A submerged platform in waters
option_3: Drifting of organisms in a body of water
option_4: A kind of animal living under water
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Plankton are a diverse group of organisms that live in water column of large bodies of water and that cannot swim against a current.
  • They provide a crucial source food to many large aquatic organisms, such as fish and whales. Plankton being weak, non-motile exist in drafting state.
  • They include mostly unicellular pro caryotes like bacteria and Eukayotos like algae, protozoa, diatoms and dino flagellates.

question: Which of the following is not favourable for the formation of deltas? (UPSC Prelims 1983)
option_1: Calm winds
option_2: Currents and tides
option_3: Sheltered coast
option_4: No large lakes in the river course
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Currents and Tides are not helpful in delta formation : Most of deltas end up forming estuaries where sea water and fresh water of rivers mix together. High tides can prevent formation of delta as low tide following high tide will not support its formation.

question: What does “Roaring Forties” refer to ? (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: The tract of stormy westerly winds between latitudes 20°-30°S
option_2: Whirl winds caused in Atlantic Ocean
option_3: The region between latitude 40°S and 50°S, where the prevailing westerly winds blow over the open oceans with great regularity and strength
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Roaring forties, areas between latitudes 40° and 50° south in the Southern Hemisphere, where the prevailing winds blow persistently from the west. The roaring forties have strong, often gale- force, winds throughout the year. They were named by the sailors who first entered these latitudes.

question: Grenada is situated in.............. (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Pacific Ocean
option_2: Indian Ocean
option_3: Caribbean Sea
option_4: Mediterranean Sea
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

Grenada is a small island nation located in the southeastern part of the Caribbean Sea, just north of Trinidad and Tobago and south of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines. It is part of the West Indies and lies within the Lesser Antilles island chain. The country is surrounded by the Caribbean Sea to the west and the Atlantic Ocean to the east. Its tropical climate, coral reefs, and volcanic origin make it a well-known tourist destination. Therefore, the correct answer is that Grenada is situated in the Caribbean Sea.

question: The North Atlantic Sea route is regarded as an important international trade route because............... (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: It is the oldest sea route
option_2: It connects two industrially developed parts of the world
option_3: Trade winds will help the shipping
option_4: There are lesser number of sea storms in this water way
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • North Atlantic Sea route is regarded as an important international trade route because it connects two industrially developed world U.K. and USA

question:
Match the following: (UPSC Prelims 1985)

A.

Argentite

1.

Copper

B.

Bauxite

2.

Iron

C.

Haematite

3.

Aluminium

D.

Cuprite

4.

Silver

Codes : A B C D
option_1: 4 3 2 1
option_2: 1 4 3 2
option_3: 2 1 4 3
option_4: 1 2 3 4
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

The Correct Answer is (A)

  • Silver is extracted from the ore-argentite. The process of extraction of silver is called as cyanide process as sodium cyanide solution is used.
  • Bauxite is primarily comprised of aluminum oxide compounds.
  • Haematite ore is mined as the main ore of iron.
  • Cuprite is an oxide mineral composed of copper oxide Cu2O, and is a minor ore of copper.

question: Trade winds are caused by the............. (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: Revolution of the earth
option_2: Rotation of the earth
option_3: In flowing movement of air along the earth’s surface towards the equator
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The Trade winds are caused by Inflowing movement of air along the earth’s surface towards the equator. Trade wind, persistent wind that blows westward and toward the Equator from the subtropical high-pressure belts toward the intertropical convergence zone (ITCZ).
  • Trade winds are caused by hot air rising at the Equator and the consequent movement of air from north and south to take its place.

question: Which of the following pairs is correct? City - River.............. (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: Baghdad - Tigris
option_2: Bonn- Danube
option_3: Rome-Seine
option_4: Paris-Tiber
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Baghdad lies on bank of river Tigris. Bonn, a city in West Germany on the bank river Rhine. Rome in central Italy is located on river Tiber and Paris in France on river Seine. Hence option (a) is correct.

question: Which of the following statements is correct? (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: Northern Ireland is included in UK and Southern Ireland is sovereign independent, democratic republic
option_2: Southern Ireland is included in UK and Northern Ireland is a sovereign independent, democratic republic
option_3: Both are sovereign, independent, democratic below republics
option_4: Both are included in U.K
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Northern Ireland is included in UK, and Southern Ireland is sovereign independent, democratic republic. Hence, A is the correct statement.

question: Ursa Minor is.............. (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: The name of an island
option_2: The name of a satellite
option_3: A group of stars that form the Little Dipper in the most northern constellation
option_4: The name of a disease in U.S.A
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Ursa Minor, also known as the Little Bear, is a constellation of stars in the Northern Sky. It is used as an indicative sign for locating Pole star”.

question: Diego Garcia is............ (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: An American military air base in the Indian Ocean
option_2: U.K. Navy base in the Pacific Ocean
option_3: An island in Antarctica
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Diego Garcia is a military base in the Indian Ocean that is leased to the United States Navy from the British Ministry of Defence. It is located on the island of Diego Garcia, which is part of the British Indian Ocean Territory.
  • The UK government forcibly removed the island’s native inhabitants in the 1960s. The US Navy has leased the island since the 1970s.

question: Isohyets are the lines drawn on a map connecting points having.............. (UPSC Prelims 1985)
option_1: Equal temperature
option_2: Equal height
option_3: Equal atmospheric pressure
option_4: Equal rainfall
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Isohyets are lines on a map connecting points having the same amount of rainfall in a given period.

question:
Match the columns : (UPSC Prelims 1985)

A. Magnesium

1.    Mantle

B. Upper layer of the earth’s crust

2.    Sima

C.    Light   rocks    of    the lithosphere

3.    Bill

D. Small Peninsula

4.    Sial

Select the correct answer from the codes given below:

A B C D
option_1: 2 3 4 1
option_2: 3 2 1 4
option_3: 1 3 4 2
option_4: 2 4 1 3
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

The Correct Answer is 2 4 1 3

  • Sima is the earth’s crusts lower layer and contains rocks with an abundance of magnesium silicate minerals, the word Sima is an amalgam of the first two letters of Magnesium and Silica.
  • In geology, the term ‘sial’ refers to the composition of the upper layer of the Earth’s crust, namely rocks rich in silicates and aluminum minerals. As these elements are less dense than the majority of the earth’s elements, they tend to be concentrated in the upper layer of the crust.
  • The crust and the upper layer of the mantle together make up a zone of rigid, brittle rock called the Lithosphere. The layer below therigid lithosphere is a zone of asphalt-like consistency called the Asthenosphere. The asthenosphere is the part of the mantle that flows and moves the plates of the Earth.
  • A peninsula is a landform surrounded by water on the majority of its border while being connected to a mainland from which it extends. Peninsulas are not always named as such; one can also be a headland, cape, island promontory, bill, point, fork, or spit.

question:
Match the columns : (UPSC Prelims 1985)

List-1

 List-11

A.Southeastern coast of U.S.A.

1. Florida current

B.West coast of South Africa

2. Kuroshio current

C.Southwestern coast of South America

3. Beneguella current

D.Southeastern coast of Japan

4. Peruvian current

Select the correct answer from the codes given

A B C D
option_1: 2 3 4 1
option_2: 1 3 4 2
option_3: 1 4 3 2
option_4: 2 4 3 1
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • The Florida Current is a thermal ocean current that flows from the Straits of Florida around the Florida Peninsula and along the southeastern coast of the United States before joining the Gulf Stream Current near Cape Hatteras. Its contributing currents are the Loop Current and the Antilles Current.
  • The Benguela Current is a cold, wide current that flows northwards along the west coast of southern Africa. It is an Eastern Boundary Current and forms the eastern flank of the South Atlantic Gyre The ocean current “begins” roughly where the eastward-flowing South Atlantic Current swings northward at the Agulhas.
  • The Peruvian Current, also called the Humboldt Current, is a cold, low-salinity ocean current that flows north along the western coast of South America. It is an eastern boundary current flowing in the direction of the equator, and extends 500 1,000 km (310–620 mi) offshore.
  • The Kuroshio a warm northeasterly ocean current off the coast of JAPAN. This current is also called the gulf stream of the Pacific or Japan Current. Kuroshio means “the black stream” in Japanese, named after the deep ultramarine color of the high salinity water, which is found flowing north of the current’s axis.

question: Pumice is derived from............ (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Volcanic rock
option_2: Sedimentary rock
option_3: Igneous rock
option_4: Acid lava
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Pumice is derived from the igneous rocks only. Hence, (C) is the correct answer.
  • Pumice is a light colored, extremely porous igneous rock that forms during explosive volcanic eruptions. It is used as aggregate in lightweight concrete, as landscaping aggregate, and as an abrasive in a variety of industrial and consumer products.

question:
Match the columns: (UPSC Prelims 1986)

A.

English

1. Israel

B.

Amharic

2. Namibia

C.

Swahili

3. Ethiopia

D.

Hebrew

4. Kenya

Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

A B C D
option_1: 3 4 1 2
option_2: 4 1 2 3
option_3: 2 4 1 3
option_4: 2 3 4 1
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

The Correct Answer is (D) 2 3 4 1.

  • The official language of Namibia is English, although this has only been the case since independence in 1990.
  • Amharic is one of the Ethiopian Semitic languages, which are a subgrouping within the Semitic branch of the Afroasiatic languages.
  • Swahili is a Bantu language spoken in Tanzania, Burundi, Congo (Kinshasa) Kenya, Mayotte, Mozambique, Oman, Rwanda, Somalia, South Africa, and Uganda.
  • Hebrew is a Northwest Semitic language native to Israel; the modern version of which is spoken by over 9 million people worldwide.

question: Mean Sea Level (MSL) is the.................. (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Average level of the sea
option_2: Height of water above ground level
option_3: Average height of waves
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Sea level is generally used to refer to mean sea level (MSL), an average level for the surface of one or more of Earth’s oceans from which heights such as elevations may be measured.

question:
Match the columns: (UPSC Prelims 1986)

A

Australia

1

Pretoria

B.

Canada

2

Riyadh

C.

South Africa

3.

Canberra

D.

Saudi Arabia

4.

Ottawa

Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

A B C D
option_1: 2 4 1 3
option_2: 3 4 1 2
option_3: 4 3 1 2
option_4: 3 4 2 1
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

The Correct Answer is (B) 3 4 1 2.

  • Pretoria is an administrative capital of South Africa.
  • Riyadh is the capital of Saudi Arabia and Riyadh Province. It is the largest city in Saudi Arabia and one of the most populated cities in the Arab world.
  • Canberra is the capital city of Australia. Founded following the federation of the colonies of Australia as the seat of government for the new nation.
  • Ottawa is the capital city of Canada. It stands on the south bank of the Ottawa River in the eastern portion of southern Ontario.

question: Massive deforestation causes................ (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Soil erosion
option_2: Famine
option_3: Floods
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Deforestation has many negative effects on the environment. The most dramatic impact is exacerbated rate of soil erosion.
  • The loss of trees, which anchor the soil with their roots, cause widespread erosion throughout the tropics. Only a minority of areas have good soils, which after clearing are quickly washed away by the heavy rains.

question: High tides at antipodes are caused due to................ (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Gravitational pull of the moon and the sun
option_2: Gravitational pull of the sun
option_3: Centrifugal, centripetal and gravitational pull of the sun
option_4: Centrifugal, centripetal and gravitational pull of the sun and the moon
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Tides are a result of gravitational pull by both Sun and Moon, but the pull exerted by Sun is apparently weak. This is becauseof the larger distance as the gravitational force is inversely proportional to the square of the distance.

question: At a certain place, the Greenwich Mean Time is ahead place is located............. (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: 180° W of GMT
option_2: 180° E of GMT
option_3: 90° W of GMT
option_4: 90° E of GMT
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

If a place is ahead of Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) by 12 hours, it means when it’s noon at Greenwich, it’s midnight of the next day at that place. The International Date Line (IDL) — roughly along the 180° meridian — divides the calendar days. The regions to the west of GMT (180° W) are ahead in time because as the Earth rotates from west to east, the western side crosses into the new calendar day first. Conversely, regions to the east of GMT (180° E) are behind by 12 hours. Therefore, a place where the local time is 12 hours ahead of GMT lies at or near the 180° W meridian, making option (a) the correct answer.

question:
Which of the following is the correct order of increasing size of the grains of soil particles? (UPSC Prelims 1986)

A. Clay

B. Silt

C. Sand

D. Gravel

(check)
option_1: A, B, C, D
option_2: D, C, A, B
option_3: B, C, A, D
option_4: C, D, B, A
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • The right order of the increasing size of soil particle is-
  1. Clay - It is the finest of all the particles then
  2. Silt - It has slightly larger particles than clay.
  3. Sand - It has bigger particles and
  4. Gravel has the largest particle size.
  • So the correct order is Clay < Silt < Sand < Gravel

question: Extrusive (Intrusive) rocks are forms of.............. (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Igneous rocks
option_2: Sedimentary rocks
option_3: Metamorphic rocks
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Extrusive and Intrusive rock systems are form of igneous rock system.
  • Igneous rocks are formed from the solidification of molten rock material (lawa).

There are two basic types :

  • Intrusive igneous rocks crystallize below Earth’s surface, and the slow cooling that occurs there allows large crystals to form. Examples of intrusive igneous rocks are diorite, gabbro, granite, pegmatite, and peridotite.
  • Extrusive igneous rocks erupt onto the surface, where they cool quickly to form small crystals. Some cool so quickly that they form an amorphous glass. These rocks include andesite, basalt, obsidian, pumice, rhyolite, scoria, and tuff.

question: Which of the following two continents are just like the each other? (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: South America and Africa
option_2: South America and North America
option_3: South America and Australia
option_4: Europe and Asia
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • South America and Africa are the two continents that are most similar in shape, with the coastlines of eastern South America and western Africa fitting together well.
  • The coastlines of the two continents match best when aligned at a depth of 1,000 meters below the current sea level.

question: The shape of the Himalayas is like................ (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: An arc
option_2: A curvilinear straight line
option_3: A straight line
option_4: A right angle
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • The Himalayas are very pointy, sharp, jagged. This is from the years of icedropping from the top slopes, carving the rock into shape. The mountains look like pyramids, triangles or sharp rocks.

question: Which of the following terms is used to denote the heavy rocks of the lithosphere? (UPSC Prelims 1986)
option_1: Substratum
option_2: SIAL
option_3: SIMA
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Sima is used to denote heavy rocks because it is denser than the Sial layer that lies above it. Sima is a layer of the Earth’s crust that is rich in silica and magnesium.
  • Sima is a layer of the Earth’s crust that is made up of silicate minerals and magnesium.
  • It is denser than the Sial layer, which is rich in silica and aluminum.
  • Sima is primarily made up of basaltic rocks.
  • Sima is found beneath the continental crust.
  • Sima is also known as the oceanic crust.
  • Sima is heavier than Sial because it has more iron and magnesium, and less aluminum.

question: The trees of spruce, fir, pine, etc. are found in which of the following natural regions of the earth? (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: Laurentian type
option_2: Alpine type
option_3: Taiga type
option_4: Siberian type
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The trees of spruce, fir, pine, etc. are found in Taiga type climate. They are gymnosperm adapted for temperate or sub-temperate conditions.

question:
Match the columns: (UPSC Prelims 1987)

A.

Groundnut

1.

Leguminosae

B.

Cotton

2.

Graminac

C.

Sugarcane

3.

Malvaceae

Select the correct answer using the codes given below :

A B C
option_1: 1 2 3
option_2: 1 3 2
option_3: 2 1 3
option_4: 3 1 2
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

Groundnut, cotton, and sugarcane each belong to different plant families based on their botanical characteristics. Groundnut belongs to the family Leguminosae (Fabaceae) because it is a legume that produces pods containing seeds and has nitrogen-fixing root nodules. Cotton belongs to the family Malvaceae, which includes plants with characteristic funnel-shaped flowers and fibrous seeds. Sugarcane belongs to the family Gramineae (Poaceae), which includes grasses and cereals that have jointed stems and parallel-veined leaves. Therefore, the correct matching is : A (Groundnut) → 1 (Leguminosae), B (Cotton) → 3 (Malvaceae), and C (Sugarcane) → 2 (Gramineae). Hence, the correct code is 1 3 2, corresponding to option (b).

question: Seismic waves that vibrate perpendicular to the direction of their path............... (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: Travel through the solid parts of the earth only
option_2: Travel through the solid as well as other parts of the earth
option_3: Spread on the surface of the earth
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

Seismic waves that vibrate perpendicular to the direction of their path are known as S-waves or secondary waves. These are transverse waves, meaning the particle motion of the medium is at right angles to the direction in which the wave travels. S-waves can only move through solid materials because their propagation requires the medium to have shear strength. Liquids and gases lack this property, so S-waves cannot travel through the earth’s outer core, which is liquid. In contrast, P-waves (primary waves) are longitudinal and can move through both solids and liquids. Therefore, seismic waves that vibrate perpendicular to their direction of travel pass only through the solid parts of the earth, making option (a) correct.

question: The largest producer of woodpulp is............... (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: Canada
option_2: USSR
option_3: U.S.A.
option_4: Japan
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The United States of America is the largest producer of woodpulp.
  • The largest producer countries, US, China, Japan and Canada, make up more than half of the world’s paper production using wood pulp which is million tonnes a year.

question: Which of the following are known as coastlines of submergence? (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: Uplifted lowland
option_2: Ria Coasts
option_3: Emergent upland
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Submerging coastline are the opposite of emergent coastlines which have experienced a relative fall in sea-levels.
  • Features of a submerging coastline are drowned river valleys or raised drowned glaciated valleys. Hence, (C) is the correct answer.

question: Physiological population density is the ratio of............ (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: Total population to total area
option_2: Total population to total irrigated land area
option_3: Total population to urban land area
option_4: Total population to rural land area
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Physiological population density is the total population divided by the total amount of arable land. It’s a ratio that’s expressed as people per square kilometer of cultivable land.

question: Of the total water on the earth, fresh water reserves constitute approximately............... (UPSC Prelims 1987)
option_1: 1.2%
option_2: 2.7%
option_3: 4.5%
option_4: 5.8%
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • According to the U.S. Geological Survey, 2.5% of the Earth’s water is freshwater. This is the amount of water needed for life to survive.

question: Seasonal migration of men and animals from lower to higher altitudes during the summer is known as............ (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Nomadism
option_2: Migration
option_3: Transhumance
option_4: Pastoralism
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The seasonal migration of people and livestock from lower to higher altitudes during the summer is called transhumance. It is a type of pastoralism or nomadism.
  • Transhumance is the seasonal movement of people and livestock between different regions.
  • It is practiced in areas with mountains, highlands, or other areas that are too cold for grazing except in summer.
  • Herders move their livestock to higher pastures in the summer and to lower valleys in the winter.
  • Herders usually have a permanent home, typically in valleys.
  • Most people who practice transhumance also cultivate crops.
  • An example of transhumance is the movement of Siberian reindeer between the subarctic taiga and the Arctic tundra.

question:
Ordinary dry air consists of the following: (UPSC Prelims 1988)

1. Nitrogen

2. Oxygen

3. Argon

4. Carbon dioxide

What is the decreasing sequence of these in percentages?
option_1: 1, 2, 3 and 4
option_2: 1, 2, 4 and 3
option_3: 2, 1, 3 and 4
option_4: 2, 1, 4 and 3
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

The Correct Answer is (A) 1 2 3 4.

  • Dry air is primarily made up of nitrogen (78.09%) and oxygen (20.95%). The remaining 1% is made up of argon (0.93%), carbon dioxide (0.039% as of 2010) and other trace gases (0.003%). Water vapour (water in its gaseous state) is also present in the atmosphere in varying amounts, by up to 2%.

question: Which one of the following is not correctly matched? (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Isopleth : A line joining places of equal height
option_2: Isobar: A line joining places of equal pressure
option_3: Isohyet : A line joining places of equal rainfall
option_4: Isotherm : A line joining places of equal temperature
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Isopleth is a line on a map connecting points having equal incidence of a specified meteorological feature. Thus, the match is incorrect.

question: Corals is/are.............. (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: A particular type of shell found near some of the seacoasts.
option_2: A special variety of fish which live in shells.
option_3: The tiny animals that live in shells in the sea.
option_4: The remains of tiny sea animals resembling shells.
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Corals are tiny animals that live in the ocean and secrete a calcium carbonate exoskeleton. They are invertebrates, meaning they don’t have a backbone.

What are corals made of?

  • Corals are made up of many individual animals called polyps. These polyps secrete calcium carbonate, a chalk-like substance, to form a rocky exoskeleton around themselves.

question: Fog is common around Newfoundland coast because................ (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Icebergs melt around the coast
option_2: Warm and cold currents meet
option_3: The rainfall is heavy
option_4: High tides cause storms
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Fog is common around Newfoundland coast due to warm and cold currents which meet here. Newfoundland and Labrador is a Canadian Atlantic province with an area of 111,390 square kilometers.
  • The province is located in eastern Canada and comprises two main parts : The island of Newfoundland and mainland Labrador, which is located northwest of the island.

question: If there were no atmosphere, what would be the colour of sky? (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: White
option_2: Black
option_3: Blue
option_4: Red
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • If there were no atmosphere, there would be no particle atoms or molecules to scatter any of the light from the sun downwards towards us and the sky would appear black (as it appears on the moon).

question:
Consider the diagram below : (UPSC Prelims 1988)

Letters A, B, C and D in the diagram represent sequential arrangement of basic topographical features along a coast. Identify the correct sequence from the codes given below :
option_1: Land, continental shelf, continental slope, deep seaplain
option_2: Land, continental slope, continental shelf, deep seaplain
option_3: Mountain, sea coast, continental slope, deep sea plain
option_4: Hill, continental slope, ocean deep, deep sea plain
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

When moving from the coast toward the deeper ocean, the seafloor shows a clear sequence of relief features. Starting from the land, the first submerged part is the continental shelf, which is a shallow, gently sloping extension of the continent under the sea. Beyond this lies the continental slope, which drops steeply down toward the ocean basin. After the slope, the sea floor becomes almost flat and extends over a vast area called the deep sea plain or abyssal plain, which forms the deepest part of the ocean basin. Hence, the correct order of the topographical features represented by A, B, C, and D is Land → Continental shelf → Continental slope → Deep sea plain, making option (a) the correct answer.

question: The vegetation of the area dotted on the map is.............. (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Pampas
option_2: Prairies
option_3: Savanna
option_4: Veld
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Savanna as tropical savanna climates are most commonly found in Africa, Asia and northern South America. The climate is also prevalent in sections of Central America, northern Australia and southern North America, specifically in sections of Mexico and the state of Florida in the United States, Most of these places are found at the outer margins of the tropical zone, but occasionally an inner tropical location (e.g. San Marcos, Antioquia, Colombia) also qualifies to be considered as savanna.

question: The climate in the shaded area of the map is of................. (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Cold temperate oceanic type
option_2: Mediterranean type
option_3: Monsoon type
option_4: Temperate Grassland type
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Mediterranean type of climate with the mediterranean forests, woodlands, and scrub biome is closely associated with mediterranean climate zones, as are unique freshwater communities. Particularly distinctive of the climate are sclerophyll shrublands, called maquis in the Mediterranean Basin, chaparral in California, matorral in Chile, fynbos in South Africa, and mallee and kwongan shrublands in Australia.

question: Which of the following rivers has a ‘bird’s foot’ delta? (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: The Amazon
option_2: The Brahmaputra
option_3: The Mississippi
option_4: The Nile
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The Mississippi River forms a bird’s foot delta. A bird’s foot delta is named for its resemblance to a bird’s foot claw.

Here is some information about the other rivers:

1. The Amazon : The Amazon Delta is a huge river delta formed by the Amazon and Tocantins rivers. It’s located in the Amazon Basin, the largest river basin on Earth.

2. The Brahmaputra : The Ganges–Brahmaputra Delta is the world’s largest delta, covering 100,000 square kilometers. It’s located in Asia, where the Ganges and Brahmaputra rivers meet the Bay of Bengal.

3. The Nile : The Nile Delta is one of the world’s largest river deltas. It covers 240 kilometers of the Mediterranean coastline, from Alexandria to Port Said.

question: The river (R) shown on the map is............... (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Niger
option_2: Orange
option_3: Congo
option_4: Zambezi
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • The Zambezi is the fourth-longest river in Africa, the longest east-flowing river in Africa and the largest flowing into the Indian Ocean from Africa. The area of its basin is 1,390,000 square kilometres, slightly less than half that of the Nile.

question: Which of the following is cold ocean current? (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: Labrador
option_2: Gulf Stream
option_3: Kuro Shio
option_4: None of these
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Labrador current is a cold oceanic current. The Labrador Current is a cold currentin the North Atlantic Ocean which flows from the Arctic Ocean, south along the coast of Labrador and passes around Newfoundland, continuing south along the east coast of Nova Scotia.

question: The international air route from West Europe to East Asia passes through India because............... (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: It is the shortest route.
option_2: India is the only country in Asia providing refueling capacity
option_3: India is a busy centre situated between the two places
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • India’s geographic location makes it to act as transit for flights connecting west Europe to East Asia. The country is situated as a busy centre situated between two important regions of the world along with refuelling capacity.

question: 12 constellations referred to as zodiac are the planets.............. (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: Imaginary region that encompass the path of planets
option_2: Signs of Roman gods
option_3: A group of stars
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

The 12 constellations known as the zodiac are a group of stars.

  • A constellation is a group of stars that form a pattern in the night sky.
  • The zodiac constellations are Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, and Pisces.
  • These constellations can be seen along the ecliptic.
  • Constellations have been used by astronomers and navigators for centuries to tell time and track the movement of celestial bodies.

question: Which of the following countries is rich in oil.............. (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: Singapore
option_2: Thailand
option_3: Pakistan
option_4: Indonesia
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Indonesia is an oil rich country. Indonesia is also a member of OPEC countries, an alliance of major oil producing countries across the world.

question: Savanna grasslands are found in................... (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: North America
option_2: Africa
option_3: Australia
option_4: East Asia
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

Savanna grasslands are most widely found in Africa, particularly in regions near the equator, between the tropical rainforests and the deserts. These grasslands are characterized by tall grasses with scattered trees and shrubs, and they experience distinct wet and dry seasons. The African Savanna, also called the Sudan type, covers large parts of countries like Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, and South Sudan. The climate here supports rich biodiversity, including animals such as lions, elephants, giraffes, and zebras. While savanna-like vegetation also exists in parts of South America and Australia, the true and most extensive savanna ecosystem is found in Africa, making option (b) the correct answer.

question: Which of the following is a block mountain? (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: Alps
option_2: Vosges
option_3: Rocky
option_4: Andes
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Vosges is a block mountain. Block Mountains or Fault Block Mountains are massive in structure. They have very steep slopes.

question: The leading producer of silver is.............. (UPSC Prelims 1989)
option_1: U.S.A.
option_2: U.S.S.R.
option_3: Mexico
option_4: Brazil
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Mexico remained the world’s largest silver producer in the world, only to be followed by China and Peru.

question: Which one of the following is not a satellite of Jupiter? (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: Lo
option_2: Callisto
option_3: Europa
option_4: Titan
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Titan is the largest moon of Saturn and NOT of Jupiter.

question: The maturity of a river course is recognised by the presence.................. (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: V-shaped valley
option_2: Meanders
option_3: Gorges
option_4: High denudational power
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • The maturity of a river course is recognized by the presence of meanders, which are winding curves in the river.

A mature river has the following characteristics:

  • Meanders: A river’s mature stage is characterized by meanders, which are winding curves in the river.
  • Wide valley floor: A mature river has a valley with a wide floor and flaring sides.
  • Smoothly graded bed: A mature river has a more smoothly graded bed.
  • Flood plains: A mature river deposits sediments, which leads to the formation of flood plains.
  • Reduced vertical cutting: A mature river’s speed reduces, leading to reduced vertical cutting and greater widening.

question: Sunda Strait separates................. (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: Burma and Celebes
option_2: Java and Sumatra
option_3: Japan and Korea
option_4: Sicily and Italy
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • The Sunda Strait is the strait between the Indonesian islands of Java and Sumatra. It connects the Java Sea to the Indian Ocean. The name comes from the Indonesian term Pasundan, meaning “West Java”

question: Caldera is a feature associated with................ (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: Volcanoes
option_2: Earthquakes
option_3: Folding of rocks
option_4: Faulting of mountains
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • A caldera is a large cauldron-like volcanic depression, a type of volcanic crater, formed by the collapse of an emptied magma chamber. The depression often originates in very big explosive eruptions.

question: Which one of the following is the largest of the inner planets? (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: Venus
option_2: Mercury
option_3: Mars
option_4: Earth
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Earth is the largest of the four inner planets in our solar system. It has an equatorial diameter of 7,926 miles (12,760 kilometers) and an equatorial radius of 6,378 kilometers (3,963 miles).
  • Earth is also the fifth largest planet in our solar system, but it’s much smaller than the gas giants in the outer solar system. Earth has the most mass and gravity of the inner planets.

question: Eskers and Drumlins are features, formed by................. (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: Running water
option_2: Underground water
option_3: The action of wind
option_4: Glacial action
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • Eskers and Drumlins both are features of glacial action landforms. However, though drumlins and eskers are both hilly glaciallandforms, they differ in shape. Eskers are narrow, long, winding hills that were deposited by streams underneath the glaciers. Drumlins, in contrast, are oval egg-like hills featuring a steep slope at one end and a gentle incline at the other.

question: If the difference in time of the two places is 2 hours and 20 minutes, then the difference in their longitudes will be............. (UPSC Prelims 1990)
option_1: 30°
option_2: 35°
option_3: 40°
option_4: 45°
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • It is given that the difference between times of two places is 2 hours and 20 minutes then, the difference in their longitudes would be 35°. Thus the correct answer is B.

question:
Match list I with list II and select the correct answer using the codes given below them : (UPSC Prelims 1990)

 

List I (City)

List II (Place)

A.

Budapest

1. Alexander Platz

B.

Warsaw

2. Walesa Square

C.

Berlin

3. Tiananmen Square

D.

Beijing

4. Ceausescu Square

 

 

5. Parliament Square

Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

A B C D
option_1: 4 3 1 5
option_2: 3 4 5 2
option_3: 5 2 4 3
option_4: 5 2 1 3
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

Budapest is paired with Parliament Square (5) because the Hungarian Parliament and its adjacent Kossuth Lajos Square (often referred to as the parliament square) are the city’s principal parliamentary landmark; Warsaw matches Walesa Square (2) named after Lech Wałęsa and located in Poland’s capital; Berlin corresponds to Alexanderplatz (1), the well-known central square in Berlin; Beijing is unmistakably linked with Tiananmen Square (3). Thus, the correct sequence is (d) 5213.

question: The light from the Sun reaches the Earth in about................ (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: 2 min
option_2: 4 min
option_3: 6 min
option_4: 8 min
solution_text:
Explanation : (D)

  • On average, it takes sunlight about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth. This is because Earth orbits the Sun at a distance of about 150 million kilometers, and light travels at 300,000 kilometers per second.
  • The Earth follows an elliptical orbit around the Sun, ranging from 147 million to 252 million kilometers. This means that when you look at the Sun, you’re actually seeing it as it was 8 minutes ago.

question: Neap tides are produced in the first and third quarters of the Moon. Then................. (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: The Moon and the Sun are in conjunction with the other planets
option_2: The Moon and the Sun are in opposition with each other
option_3: The attractions of the Sun and the Moon are at right angles to each other
option_4: The Moon and the Sun are in quadrant position to each other
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • A neap tide-seven days after a spring tide refers to a period of moderate tides when the sun and moon are at right angles to each other.

question: Density of population refers to................ (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: The number of persons living per sq km of land area
option_2: The number of persons living per km of land area
option_3: The number of persons living per village
option_4: None of the above
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Population density is a measurement of population per unit area or unit volume; it is a quantity of type number density. It is frequently applied to living organisms, and most of the time to humans.

question: Terrace cultivation is practiced mostly............ (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: In urban areas
option_2: On slopes of mountains
option_3: On tops of hills
option_4: In undulating tracts
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Graduated terrace cultivation is commonly used to farm on hilly or mountainous terrain. Terraced fields decrease both erosion and surface runoff, and may be used to support growing crops that require irrigation, such as rice

question:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the Lists: (UPSC Prelims 1991)

List I  Measuring  instruments

List II  Which Measure?

A. Anemometer

1. Motor power

B. Tachometer

2. Wind speed

C. Dynamometer

3. Revolutions per minute

D. Barometer

4. Atmospheric pressure

 

5. Current from a dynamo

Select the correct answer from the codes given below:

A B C D
option_1: 1 3 5 4
option_2: 2 1 3 4
option_3: 2 3 1 4
option_4: 1 3 5 2
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • Barometer is used for measuring atmospheric pressure.
  • Dynamometers can measure the power, torque, and speed of engines, motors, and other rotating machines.
  • Anemometer is used to measure wind speed.
  • Tachometer measures the speed of a rotating object, such as a shaft or disk, in revolutions per minute (RPM)

question: Seasons on the Earth are caused due to.............. (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: The elliptical orbit of the Earth
option_2: Alteration/change in the speed of revolution of the Earth
option_3: Inclination of the Earth on its axis of rotation
option_4: Differences in the amount of energy received from the Sun
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  • The seasons are caused by the tilt of the Earth’s rotational axis away or toward the sun as it travels through its year-long path around the sun. The Earth has a tilt of degrees relative to the “ecliptic plane” (the imaginary surface formed by it’s almost cicular path around the sun).
  • The tilt on its rotational axis leads to opposite development of climatic conditions in northern and southern hemisphere, one hemisphere being closer and other being away from the sun.

question: Barkhan (Barchans) is a.............. (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: Crescent shaped sand dune
option_2: Depositional bar, separated by water from the coast
option_3: Dark coloured, fine grained rock
option_4: Glacial erosional feature
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • Barchan dune (bär-kin’) A large, crescent shaped dune lying at right angles to the prevailing wind and having a steep, concave leeward side with the crescent tips pointing downwind. Barchan dunes are normallyformed on flat, hard surfaces where the sand supply is limited.

question: Jupiter completes one circuit of the Sun in.............. (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: 13 years
option_2: 12 years
option_3: 16 years
option_4: 10 years
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • Jupiter takes about 12 Earth years, or 4,333 Earth days, to complete one orbit around the Sun.
  • Jupiter’s distance from the Sun makes its orbital period much longer than Earth’s. Jupiter is a gas giant with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets in the Solar System combined. It orbits the Sun at a distance of 5.20 AU (778.5 Gm).
  • Jupiter also has the shortest day in the solar system, rotating once every 10 hours.

question:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the Lists: (UPSC Prelims 1991)

 

List I:

 

List II

A.

Deforestation

1.

Vacuoles

B.

Cellular Structure

2.

Maize

C.

Cross Pollination

3.

Soil Erosion

D.

Nitrogen fixation

4.

Legumes

Select the correct answer from the codes given below.

A B C D
option_1: 3 1 2 4
option_2: 1 2 4 3
option_3: 4 1 2 3
option_4: 2 3 4 1
solution_text:
Explanation : (A)

  • The loss of trees, which anchor the soil with their roots, causes widespread erosion throughout the tropics.
  • Vacuoles are storage bubbles found in cells. They are found in both animal and plantcells but are much larger in plant cells. Vacuoles might store food or any variety of nutrients a cell might need to survive. They can even store waste products so the rest of the cell is protected from contamination.
  • Maize is a wind-pollinating crop and is able to cross-pollinate in neighboring fields.
  • In legumes and a few other plants, the bacteria live in small growths on the roots called nodules. Within these nodules, nitrogen fixation is done by the bacteria, and the NH3 they produce is absorbed by the plant. Nitrogen fixation by legumes is a partnership between a bacterium and a plant. So, the correct answer is ‘A - 3, B - 1, C - 2, D - 4’.

question: The speed of revolution of the earth in its orbit round the sun is................ (UPSC Prelims 1991)
option_1: Same throughout the year
option_2: Greatest when the earth is nearest to the sun
option_3: Greatest when the earth is farthest from the sun
option_4: Least when the earth is nearest to the sun
solution_text:
Explanation : (B)

  • The speed of Earth’s revolution around the Sun is greatest when the Earth is closest to the Sun, which is called perihelion.
  • Earth’s orbit around the Sun is elliptical, not circular, so its speed is not constant. When Earth is at perihelion, it’s closer to the Sun and moves faster to cover the greater distance in a shorter time.
  • When Earth is at aphelion, it’s farthest from the Sun and moves slower.
  • Kepler’s second law of planetary motion states that the line joining the Sun to a planet sweeps out equal areas in equal intervals of time. This means that the planet’s areal velocity is constant. .
